title: Buttery Dutch Onion Bake
description: A delicious, halal-friendly Dutch dish perfect for a lazy Sunday. The buttery glaze adds a crispy texture to the yellow onion.
instructions: In a large skillet, melt 1/4 cup of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 8 minutes.
In a separate bowl, mix together the flour, basil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Add the flour mixture to the skillet with the onion and cook for 1 minute.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, milk, and a pinch of salt.
Pour the milk mixture over the onion mixture in the skillet and cook until the mixture thickens, about 2 minutes.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy.
While the bake is in the oven, melt the remaining 1/4 cup of butter in a saucepan over low heat.
Remove the bake from the oven and brush the melted butter over the top.
Sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese over the top of the bake and return to the oven for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
